name: Quality Tier 1 — single table, no joins
description: Shape: SELECT/COUNT/FILTER on a single table. Each scenario carries the expected scalar answer (or expected row count) so verdict=OK can be checked for SEMANTIC correctness, not just SQL validity. Computed against the live DB on 2026-05-25 before this suite was written.

Scenarios.Question: how many customers
Scenarios.Notes: Single COUNT(*) on Customers. No joins, no filters. Verifies the pure-count code path.

Scenarios.Question: how many bills are there in total
Scenarios.Notes: Largest domain table. Verifies counting on a high-row-count table.

Scenarios.Question: how many bills have been paid
Scenarios.Notes: COUNT with a Status='Paid' filter. Tests the structural rule 'add filter, keep select empty'.

Scenarios.Question: how many overdue bills do we have
Scenarios.Notes: Same shape as QT1-CNT-PAID but with a different lookup value to detect over-fitting.

Scenarios.Question: list all active service types
Scenarios.ExpectedShape: SELECT label columns, no aggregation, soft-delete filter applied
Scenarios.Notes: Verifies the 'list X' shape with the soft-delete IsActive=1 filter auto-injected.

Scenarios.Question: show me all the regions
Scenarios.ExpectedShape: SELECT label columns, no aggregation, no soft-delete (table has none)
Scenarios.Notes: Regions has no IsActive column. Verifies the compiler doesn't synthesize a phantom soft-delete filter.
